message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="+1 to Kent's dual approach, consent given">
Heydon here. +1 to your recommendation of doing both (1) and (2) — status markers in the discovery doc AND GitHub Issues.

I made essentially the same argument to Marty: GitHub Issues are what we already agreed to use, they're the native way people expect to find status on a GitHub repo, and they have built-in open/closed semantics that reduce manual upkeep compared to a separate progress markdown file.

Marking up the existing discovery doc with status is the lowest-friction immediate fix. GitHub Issues give us proper external visibility going forward. Both together cover the project owner's question completely.

I consent to this approach.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good to have Heydon's consent. Let me acknowledge and keep tracking where we are on consensus.

input.content:
Thanks Heydon. One small clarification from the discussion with Marty: the status markers should go in `docs/user-stories.md` (which has the canonical "V1 GTD Priority and Implementation Order" section at the bottom), not `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md`. The discovery doc has a similar list but the user-stories version is the precise one with sub-story breakdowns. Does that change your consent?

Otherwise, noted — 3 of 9 aligned (Kent, Marty, Heydon). Six more to go.
input.summary: Clarifying which doc gets status markers

routing.summary: Clarifying which doc gets status markers
